english: = rṅán-pa (rngan pa) sbst. Glr., vb. Lex.
1. Sch. = bnád-pa (bnad pa). - 2. Lex. = rnáb-pa (rnab pa), rṅáms-pa (rngams pa).
to be faint or exhausted Cs.; v. sdug-bsṅál (sdug bsngal).
to place upon a cushion Sch.
1. v. sṅó-ba (sngo ba). - 2. a blessing, cf. sṅó-ba (sngo ba). - 3. Cs. also: mouldy, rotten (prob. only livid, discoloured, v. sṅo (sngo)).

1. the letter ċ (ca), tenuis, palatal, like the Italian ci in ciascuno, or c in cicerone. - 2. as numerical figure: 5. - 3. = lċa (lca) excrement, alvine discharges, ċa dór-ba (ca dor ba) to discharge excrements Mil.
lark Ld.
warped, distorted, awry Sch.

clamour, cries, snyiṅ tsim-gyi ċa-ċo (snying tsim gyi ca co) shout, exclamation of joy Pth.; noise, of many people Thgy.; da ċa-ċò ma zer (da ca co ma zer) now do not make such a noise! (so Mil. rebukes the aërial spirits); chirping, twitter Glr.; ċá-ċò-ċan (ca co can) shouting, bawling; talkative, loquacious Stg.
also tsá-dar (tsa dar), tsá-sar (tsa sar), a sheet, blanket, toga.
or ċi-ri-rí (ci ri ri), W. *ċár-pa ċa-ra-rá yoṅ dug* (car pa ca ra ra yong dug), it rains heavily, it is pouring.
W. bug.
continually, always = ċar (car).
termination of the plur. of pers. pronouns.
cartilage, gristle; snai ċag-krúm (snai cag krum) bridge of the nose.
W. quartz.

C. *ċág-ga jhè'-pa* (cag ga jhe' pa), = nyá-ra byéd-pa (nya ra byed pa), C. la (la), to take care of; *ċág-ga dág-po jhè'-pa* (cag ga dag po jhe' pa) to look after, to keep, preserve carefully; *ċág-ga dág-po* (cag ga dag po) careful, orderly, regular, tidy, of persons.
smacking in eating Cs.
closely pressed or crowded, in standing or sitting Ld.
= ċag-dkár (cag dkar) W.

(v. ċiaṅ (ciang), ċi-yaṅ (ci yang)), every thing, any thing whatever, ċaṅ-sès (cang ses) knowing every thing, epithet of deities or saints; more frq. followed by a negative particle and then signifying: nothing; *ċaṅ mi sto* (cang mi sto) it does not matter, it is indifferent (to me), frq.; *ċaṅ med* (cang med) there is nothing here, or at hand; also = *ċaṅ mi sto* (cang mi sto); *ċaṅ mi śès-kan* (cang mi shes kan) ignorant, stupid; blockhead, simpleton.
Glr., also ċaṅ-ċaṅ-téu (cang cang te'u) a sort of small drum Pth.

affix, adjective termination, prop. signifying: having, being provided with, = daṅ ldán-pa (dang ldan pa), corresponding to the English adj. terminations -ous, -y, -ly, -ful, e.g. tsér-ma-ċan (tser ma can) thorny; sometimes also = -like or -ish: bón-ċan (bon can) Bon-like, heretical Mil., hin-du-ċan (hin du can) Hindoo-like, Hindooish; seldom affixed to verbs: byéd-pa-ċan (byed pa can) a doer, maker; in C. also for the possessive pron.: ṅá-ċan (nga can), kó-ċan (ko can), my, his (her), *nag-gòṅ sà-hib-ċen* (nag gong sa hib cen) the Sahib's inkstand. It may also be affixed to a set of words that form one expression: tsér-ma nón-po-ċan (tser ma non po can) having sharp thorns, séṅ-gei mgó-ċan (seng gei mgo can) having a lion's head.
